utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items requested in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. In the final coverage plan, these five items all appear. Next, check if each is fully specified with all details (i.e., includes the type and costs). Health coverage: includes physical and mental health insurance with monthly and annual costs—fully specified. Property coverage: home/contents insurance with monthly and annual costs—fully specified. Travel coverage: travel insurance with monthly and annual costs—fully specified. Additional services: enhancement services with monthly and annual costs—fully specified. Total budget: monthly and annual cost breakdown—fully specified. Although there is an extra category (coverage_for_adult_children), that does not affect the count for the required format. All 5 required items are present and include costs.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: We identify each named plan in the final coverage plan and find the exact matching item in the ground-truth ratings. Mappings: Health physical uses LifeFirst Standard Plan (physical_health) and mental uses PrimeCare Premium Plan (mental_health). Property uses PrimeHome Premium Coverage. Travel includes EliteTravel Premium Plan for Lucas and an add-on 'Medical Emergency Plus' for Isabella, but 'Medical Emergency Plus' is not present in ground-truth; only EliteTravel Premium Plan is mappable. Additional services include Premium Insurance Membership, Insurance Portfolio Management, and Claims Advocacy Service, all present in general_services. Executive Insurance Concierge is not in ground-truth. Coverage for adult children references 'EliteCare Premium Plan' tiers; these map to primary_health physical (EliteCare Premium Plan) and mental_health (EliteCare Premium Plan).
